previous_statement: (Step 1: Face-commutant gives geometric-algebraic bridge). By Node 1.1 (Alfsen-Shultz face-commutant correspondence): dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) = dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) - 1. This converts the geometric quantity (face dimension) into an algebraic quantity (commutant dimension).
new_statement: (Step 1: Face-commutant gives geometric-algebraic bridge — amended). By Node 1.3.1, the hypotheses of Node 1.1 are satisfied (A is in the applicable scope, GNS yields bounded representations). Therefore, by Node 1.1 (Alfsen-Shultz face-commutant correspondence): dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) = dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) - 1. This converts the geometric quantity (face dimension in the state space) into the algebraic quantity (real dimension of the self-adjoint part of the commutant). This is a deduction by universal instantiation of Node 1.1, depending on Nodes 1.1 and 1.3.1.
